canonical_definition: The Human ChatGPT Comparison Corpus (HC3) is predominantly used as a benchmark for evaluating machine-generated text detection. Across the provided literature, it is consistently described as a dataset containing paired responses from both humans and ChatGPT (specifically GPT-3.5) to the same questions, often in a Q&A format. A frequently highlighted feature is its multiscale design, offering both full-text and sentence-level variants in English and Chinese to assess "multiscale detection performance." The short-text versions are specifically named "HC3-English-Sent and HC3-Chinese-Sent." While the prevailing application is for human-AI text discrimination tasks, such as training an encoder on its human-written texts, a less common use is also documented. One paper employs HC3 not for simple detection but to "validate... differences between ChatGPT and human responses" and compare their qualitative characteristics.
papers: Active Layer-Contrastive Decoding Reduces Hallucination in Large Language Model Generation
CAT: Causal Attention Tuning For Injecting Fine-grained Causal Knowledge into Large Language Models
Detecting Machine-Generated Texts by Multi-Population Aware Optimization for Maximum Mean Discrepancy
Multiscale Positive-Unlabeled Detection of AI-Generated Texts
Deep Kernel Relative Test for Machine-generated Text Detection
VibeCheck: Discover and Quantify Qualitative Differences in Large Language Models

definitions.text: Up-to-date ChatGPT text detection dataset including both English and Chinese, with full and sentence-level (short-text) variants for evaluating multiscale detection performance.
definitions.paper: Detecting Machine-Generated Texts by Multi-Population Aware Optimization for Maximum Mean Discrepancy

definitions.text: The Human ChatGPT Comparison Corpus (HC3) is a dataset containing responses from both humans and GPT-3.5 to the same questions, used for comparing their characteristics.
definitions.paper: VibeCheck: Discover and Quantify Qualitative Differences in Large Language Models
